decisions.title: V1 MVP deadline
decisions.detail: A working MVP for V1 must be delivered by September 1st.

evidence.quote: The main goal for V1 is a working MVP by September 1st.

decisions.title: V1 deadline is a hard constraint
decisions.detail: The V1 deadline is a hard constraint due to a marketing campaign launch on September 5th.

evidence.quote: We have a hard constraint on the deadline because of the marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: Ads for marketing campaign booked
decisions.detail: Ads for the marketing campaign launching September 5th have already been booked.

evidence.quote: Yes, we've already booked the ads.

decisions.title: V1 core features
decisions.detail: V1 must include user authentication and a working project dashboard.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication and a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: System performance
decisions.detail: The system should be "super fast".

evidence.quote: Let's make sure it's super fast.

decisions.title: Frontend technology
decisions.detail: React will be used for the frontend.

evidence.quote: we are using React for the frontend

decisions.title: Backend technology
decisions.detail: Python (FastAPI) will be used for the backend service.

evidence.quote: Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Rationale for Python backend
decisions.detail: Python was chosen for the backend due to existing team expertise and libraries.

evidence.quote: I think it makes the most sense for our team since we already have libraries built in Python.

decisions.title: Database choice
decisions.detail: Question regarding the choice of database.

evidence.quote: What about the database?

decisions.title: Database technology
decisions.detail: PostgreSQL will be used as the database, using a standard relational model.

evidence.quote: We'll use PostgreSQL. Standard relational model is fine.

decisions.title: Session management technology
decisions.detail: Question about whether to use Redis or standard database sessions for session management.

evidence.quote: what about session management? Should we use Redis or just standard database sessions?

decisions.title: Session management decision
decisions.detail: Redis is deferred to V2 and will only be considered earlier if it becomes a performance blocker. Standard PostgreSQL sessions will be used for now to save budget.

evidence.quote: Let's defer Redis to V2 unless it's a performance blocker. Keep it simple. Let's stick to standard postgres sessions for now to save budget.

decisions.title: User authentication implementation method
decisions.detail: Question about the method for implementing user authentication.

evidence.quote: How are we doing user auth?

decisions.title: Do not build user authentication in-house
decisions.detail: The team should not build user authentication themselves; external services like Auth0 or Firebase Auth should be used.

evidence.quote: We don't want to build it ourselves, right?

decisions.title: User authentication service for V1
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be integrated for V1, chosen for its speed, social login capabilities, and pricing.

evidence.quote: for V1, let's just integrate Firebase Authentication. It's quick, handles social logins easily, and matches our pricing requirements.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth login methods
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be configured to allow Google sign-in and email logins.

evidence.quote: We will configure it to allow Google sign-in and email logins.

decisions.title: User registration acceptance criteria and fields
decisions.detail: Question regarding the acceptance criteria for user registration and the specific fields to be collected.

evidence.quote: Do we have acceptance criteria for user registration? What fields do we collect?

decisions.title: User registration fields (initial)
decisions.detail: Initially, user registration should collect email, full name, and role. Address and telephone number are not required.

evidence.quote: Just email, full name, and role. We don't need address or telephone number for now.

decisions.title: Company name required for B2B
decisions.detail: Company name must be collected during signup because the project is B2B.

evidence.quote: we need to collect company name too! We are B2B.

decisions.title: User registration fields (final)
decisions.detail: Company name is required during user signup, in addition to email, full name, and role.

evidence.quote: Company name is required during signup.

decisions.title: Proposed deployment platforms
decisions.detail: John proposed Cloud Run for the FastAPI backend and Vercel for the React frontend.

evidence.quote: What about deployment? I was thinking Cloud Run for the FastAPI app and Vercel for the React app.

decisions.title: Frontend deployment platform
decisions.detail: Vercel is accepted as the deployment platform for the React frontend.

evidence.quote: Vercel is fine

decisions.title: Cloud Run security concern
decisions.detail: A question was raised about the security of Cloud Run for the backend.

evidence.quote: is Cloud Run secure?

decisions.title: Database security via private VPC
decisions.detail: The database will be placed in a private VPC and connected to Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access, ensuring the PostgreSQL instance is not exposed to the public internet.

evidence.quote: we will place the database in a private VPC and connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access. That way the postgres instance is not exposed to the public internet.

decisions.title: Backend deployment platform
decisions.detail: Cloud Run will be used for the FastAPI backend, with a specific security configuration.

evidence.quote: I was thinking Cloud Run for the FastAPI app
Yes, we will place the database in a private VPC and connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access.

decisions.title: Security constraint for database exposure
decisions.detail: Preventing the PostgreSQL instance from being exposed to the public internet is a strict technical constraint for security.

evidence.quote: This is a strict technical constraint for security.

decisions.title: Thorough testing of auth flow
decisions.detail: The authentication flow needs to be tested thoroughly.

evidence.quote: We need to test the auth flow thoroughly.

decisions.title: User session duration
decisions.detail: Question about the duration of a user session after login.

evidence.quote: If a user logs in, how long does the session last?

decisions.title: Token expiration duration
decisions.detail: Token expiration will be set to 24 hours, with the possibility of adjustment later.

evidence.quote: Let's set token expiration to 24 hours. We can adjust it later.

decisions.title: Dashboard metrics
decisions.detail: Question about which metrics will be displayed on the project dashboard.

evidence.quote: What about the dashboard? What metrics are we displaying?

decisions.title: Dashboard required metrics
decisions.detail: The dashboard needs to display active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

decisions.title: Defer dashboard feature detailing
decisions.detail: Detailed dashboard features will be discussed in the next sync meeting due to time constraints.

evidence.quote: Let's detail the dashboard features in the next sync. We are running out of time.

decisions.title: Firebase provisioning ownership
decisions.detail: Question regarding who is responsible for Firebase provisioning and when the config keys are needed.

evidence.quote: who is handling the Firebase provisioning? I need the config keys by next week.

decisions.title: Assign Firebase provisioning to Mike
decisions.detail: Mike will be assigned to handle Firebase provisioning.

evidence.quote: I'll assign that to Mike.

decisions.title: Firebase config keys deadline
decisions.detail: Mike is responsible for Firebase provisioning and should have the config keys ready by July 8th.

evidence.quote: He should have it ready by July 8th.

decisions.title: Sync with marketing team on dashboard design
decisions.detail: A sync meeting with the marketing team on the dashboard design should happen next Wednesday.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure we sync with the marketing team on the dashboard design next Wednesday.

implementation_scope: Integrate Firebase Authentication configured for Google sign-in and email logins.
Develop user registration flow to collect email, full name, role, and company name.
Implement a project dashboard displaying active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Implement data storage using PostgreSQL with a standard relational model.
Implement session management using standard PostgreSQL sessions.
Configure token expiration for user sessions to 24 hours.

constraints.text: V1 MVP must be delivered by September 1st (hard deadline due to marketing campaign launch on September 5th).
User authentication must not be built in-house; external services must be used.
PostgreSQL database instance must not be exposed to the public internet.
Database must be placed in a private VPC and connected to Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access.
Firebase config keys are needed by July 8th (owned by Mike).

architectural_decisions.text: Database: PostgreSQL with a standard relational model.
Session management: Standard PostgreSQL sessions (Redis deferred to V2 unless performance becomes a blocker).
User authentication service: Firebase Authentication for V1.
Detailed dashboard features are deferred to a future sync meeting.

acceptance_criteria: Given a user navigates to the login page, When they choose "Google Sign-in", Then they shall be successfully authenticated via Firebase Authentication and redirected to the project dashboard.

Given a user navigates to the login page, When they provide valid email and password, Then they shall be successfully authenticated via Firebase Authentication and redirected to the project dashboard.
Given a user is authenticated, When 24 hours pass without activity, Then their session token shall expire, requiring re-authentication for subsequent actions.

Given a new user attempts to register, When they provide a valid Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name, Then their account shall be successfully created via Firebase Authentication, and they shall be logged in.
Given a new user attempts to register, When they omit any required field (Email, Full Name, Role, Company Name), Then registration shall fail, and an appropriate error message shall be displayed.
Given a user is logged in, When they access the project dashboard, Then the dashboard shall display active user metrics.
Given a user is logged in, When they access the project dashboard, Then the dashboard shall display system status information.
Given a user is logged in, When they access the project dashboard, Then the dashboard shall display a list of projects.
test_scenarios: Verify successful login using Google sign-in for a new user, ensuring they are redirected to the dashboard.
Verify successful login using Google sign-in for an existing user, ensuring they are redirected to the dashboard.

Verify successful registration and login using email/password for a new user, ensuring all required fields (Email, Full Name, Role, Company Name) are collected and the user is redirected to the dashboard.
Verify successful login using email/password for an existing user, ensuring they are redirected to the dashboard.
Verify failed login attempts with an unregistered email address, displaying an appropriate error message.
Verify failed login attempts with a registered email but incorrect password, displaying an appropriate error message.
Verify password reset flow for email/password authentication (if available through Firebase Auth UI).
Verify error handling for registration attempts with an email address already in use.
Verify error handling for registration attempts with invalid email formats.
Verify that after a user logs in, their session persists for actions taken within 24 hours.
Verify that a user's session expires and requires re-authentication if they remain inactive for longer than 24 hours.
Verify that the project dashboard loads successfully for an authenticated user.
Verify that active user metrics are displayed and appear up-to-date on the project dashboard.
Verify that system status information is displayed and appears up-to-date on the project dashboard.
Verify that a list of projects is displayed on the project dashboard.
edge_cases: Attempt user registration and login with maximum length inputs for Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name fields (if character limits are defined by Firebase or the application).
Attempt user registration and login with special characters or unicode characters in Full Name, Role, and Company Name fields.
Test concurrency: Multiple users attempting to register or log in simultaneously.
Observe system behavior during and after a simulated outage or degraded performance of Firebase Authentication.
Verify system behavior when a user's local client clock is significantly out of sync with the server, specifically around token expiration.
Perform load testing to ensure key user flows (login, dashboard access) remain performant under expected and peak user loads, aiming for "super fast" responsiveness.
unresolved_behaviors: What are the specific, measurable performance targets for "super fast" system performance (e.g., maximum response times for user authentication, dashboard load, concurrent user capacity)? (Ownership: PM/QA)

Beyond "active user metrics, system status, and project lists", what are the detailed requirements and specific data points to be displayed for each section on the project dashboard (e.g., specific metrics for active users, types of system status, project attributes in the list)? (Ownership: PM/QA)

decision_history: A working MVP for V1 must be delivered by September 1st, which is a hard constraint due to a marketing campaign launch on September 5th.
V1 core features must include user authentication and a working project dashboard.
The system should aim to be "super fast".
React will be used for the frontend.
Python (FastAPI) will be used for the backend service, chosen due to existing team expertise and libraries.
PostgreSQL will be used as the database, using a standard relational model.
Standard PostgreSQL sessions will be used for session management for V1 to save budget; Redis is deferred to V2 unless it becomes a performance blocker.
User authentication will not be built in-house; external services will be used.
Firebase Authentication will be integrated for V1, chosen for its speed, social login capabilities, and pricing.
Firebase Authentication will be configured to allow Google sign-in and email logins.
User registration will initially collect email, full name, role, and company name (required for B2B). Address and telephone number are not required.
Vercel is accepted as the deployment platform for the React frontend.

Cloud Run will be used for the FastAPI backend. The PostgreSQL instance will be placed in a private VPC and connected to Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access to prevent public internet exposure (a strict security constraint).
The authentication flow needs to be tested thoroughly.
Token expiration will be set to 24 hours, with the possibility of adjustment later.
The dashboard needs to display active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Detailed dashboard features will be discussed in the next sync meeting due to time constraints.
Mike will be assigned to handle Firebase provisioning.
dependencies: The project MVP delivery by September 1st is critical, as ads for the marketing campaign launching September 5th have already been booked (External Marketing Team).
A sync meeting with the marketing team on the dashboard design should happen next Wednesday (Internal Marketing Team).
risks: Hard deadline for V1 MVP on September 1st (4 days before marketing launch) means any delays will critically impact the marketing campaign and business objectives (Likelihood: High, Impact: High).
Vague performance requirement: 'System should be super fast' is not quantifiable, risking scope creep or unmet expectations (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium).

Deferring Redis for session management to V2 for budget reasons introduces a risk that standard PostgreSQL sessions might become a performance blocker for V1 (Likelihood: Low for MVP, Impact: High if it occurs).

Deferral of detailed dashboard feature discussion to the next sync meeting could delay requirements clarification for a core V1 feature, potentially impacting development timeline or leading to rework (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium).
alignment_gaps: Lack of quantified performance metrics for the 'super fast' requirement may lead to differing interpretations and expectations among stakeholders.
Detailed features and design for the project dashboard are yet to be fully defined, which could lead to misalignment between development and marketing expectations.
open_questions: What are the complete acceptance criteria for user registration, beyond the fields already defined (email, full name, role, company name)? (PM/QA)
What are the detailed features and specific design requirements for the V1 project dashboard, beyond the high-level metrics identified? (PM/Marketing)
milestones: V1 MVP must be delivered by 2026-09-01.
Mike to provide Firebase config keys by 2026-07-08.
Sync with Marketing team on dashboard design by next Wednesday.

system_requirements: The system SHALL deliver a working MVP for V1 by September 1st (inferred from D001).
The system SHALL include user authentication as a core V1 feature (D004).
The system SHALL include a working project dashboard as a core V1 feature (D004).
The system SHALL exhibit high performance, perceived as 'super fast' (D005).
Firebase Authentication SHALL be configured to allow Google sign-in (D016).
Firebase Authentication SHALL be configured to allow email logins (D016).
User registration SHALL collect email, full name, and role (D018).
User registration SHALL collect company name (D019, D020).
User registration SHALL NOT require address or telephone number (D018).
The system SHALL ensure the authentication flow is thoroughly tested (D027).
The project dashboard SHALL display active user metrics (D031).
The project dashboard SHALL display system status (D031).
The project dashboard SHALL display project lists (D031).
data_flows: Frontend (React) to Firebase Authentication: User authentication requests (login, registration, Google Sign-in).
Firebase Authentication to Frontend (React): Authentication tokens and user profile data.
Frontend (React) to Backend (FastAPI): Authenticated API requests using tokens.
Backend (FastAPI) to PostgreSQL Database: Data persistence for application data and session information.
PostgreSQL Database to Backend (FastAPI): Retrieval of application data and session information.
Backend (FastAPI) to Firebase Authentication: (Inferred) Token validation and potentially user data lookup post-authentication.
interface_contracts: Firebase Authentication API: Endpoints for user registration (inputs: email, full name, role, company name), user login (email/password, Google OAuth), token issuance.
Firebase Authentication API: Output for successful authentication (ID Token, Refresh Token, User Profile information).
Backend (FastAPI) API: Authentication endpoints for receiving and validating Firebase ID Tokens from the frontend.
Backend (FastAPI) API: User management endpoints for creating/updating user profiles in PostgreSQL based on Firebase data (inferred).
Backend (FastAPI) API: Dashboard endpoints for retrieving active user metrics, system status, and project lists (inferred).

constraints.text: The V1 MVP must be delivered by September 1st due to a hard dependency on a marketing campaign launching September 5th for which ads are already booked.
The team SHALL NOT build user authentication in-house; an external service must be integrated.
The PostgreSQL instance MUST NOT be exposed to the public internet.

architectural_decisions.text: The frontend will be developed using React.
The backend service will be developed using Python with FastAPI framework.
PostgreSQL will be used as the relational database.
Session management will initially use standard PostgreSQL sessions, deferring Redis to V2 unless performance becomes a blocker (budget constraint).
Firebase Authentication will be integrated for V1 user authentication.
The React frontend will be deployed on Vercel.
The FastAPI backend will be deployed on Cloud Run, with the database connected via Serverless VPC Access for security.
Authentication token expiration will be set to 24 hours.
Detailed dashboard features will be deferred for discussion in a subsequent meeting.

open_analysis_questions: How will the Firebase Authentication tokens (ID Token, Refresh Token) be managed across the React frontend and FastAPI backend to maintain user sessions and implement the 24-hour token expiration?

What are the specific data points and calculations required for 'active user metrics', 'system status', and 'project lists' to be displayed on the dashboard? (Blocked by: further detailing of dashboard features, owned by PM/QA - D032, D030)
What are the specific security configurations and best practices that need to be implemented for the Cloud Run backend, beyond the database VPC connection, to ensure overall system security?

stakeholder.business_summary: The team is actively working towards delivering a functional version of our product (V1 MVP) by September 1st, a critical deadline aligned with a marketing campaign launch on September 5th. Significant progress has been made on defining core features like user authentication and a basic project dashboard, and key technology decisions have been confirmed.
key_impact: The product will be ready to support the pre-booked marketing campaign, ensuring we meet a crucial market launch date and maximize its impact.
Users will benefit from a secure and 'super fast' system with easy sign-in options, enhancing their overall experience.
Initial user registration will efficiently collect essential B2B information, including company name, to better serve our business clients.
Strategic technology choices and leveraging existing team expertise are helping to manage budget and accelerate development timelines.
decisions_needing_alignment: Alignment is needed on the specific, detailed features and overall design of the project dashboard, particularly with the marketing team. While core metrics have been identified, the full scope requires stakeholder sign-off to proceed effectively.
major_unresolved_questions: Are there any specific user acceptance criteria or processes, beyond collecting email, full name, role, and company name, that need to be defined for the new user registration?

issues.description: The test scenario 'Verify password reset flow for email/password authentication (if available through Firebase Auth UI)' introduces a feature (password reset flow) that was not explicitly defined as a requirement or decision for V1 in the extracted decisions (D016 only mentions email logins). While Firebase Auth generally supports this, its inclusion as a required test scenario overstates the confirmed scope for the current MVP.
issues.suggestion: Clarify with the PM whether password reset is a V1 requirement. If not, consider moving this to a future release or making it conditional/optional, and remove or rephrase the test scenario to reflect only confirmed features.

issues.description: The specific year '2026' for milestones ('V1 MVP must be delivered by 2026-09-01.', 'Mike to provide Firebase config keys by 2026-07-08.') is an inference not present in the extracted decisions (D001, D035), which only stated the day and month.
issues.suggestion: Remove the inferred year (e.g., 'V1 MVP must be delivered by September 1st.') or add a note indicating the year is based on the current project context if it cannot be explicitly sourced.

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are well-structured and largely accurate, with claims traceable to the extracted decisions. Two minor issues were identified: a potential overstatement of scope in the QA view regarding password reset flow, and an inferred year in the PM view's milestones. Neither of these are critical issues that block publishing.
